definition.q: Abortion is defined as termination of pregnancy before:
definition.explain: Abortion is termination of pregnancy before viability of the fetus, i.e. before 20 weeks.

definition.q: The most common cause of early spontaneous abortion is:
definition.explain: Chromosomal abnormalities account for approximately 50% of early abortions.

threatened.q: The defining feature of threatened abortion is:
options: Closed cervix with a living fetus on ultrasound
threatened.explain: Threatened abortion is characterized by slight vaginal bleeding, absent or slight pain, closed cervix, and a living fetus on ultrasound.

threatened.q: Management of threatened abortion includes all EXCEPT:
threatened.explain: Threatened abortion is managed conservatively with bed rest, avoiding strenuous activity and intercourse, progesterone, and follow-up. Evacuation is not indicated.

options: Heavy vaginal bleeding with clots and dilated cervix
inevitable.explain: Inevitable abortion presents with heavy vaginal bleeding with clots, suprapubic colic, and a dilated cervix — the pregnancy cannot be saved.

complete.q: In complete abortion, the uterus on ultrasound shows:
complete.explain: In complete abortion, all products of conception have been expelled, so ultrasound shows an empty cavity.

options: Ecbolics and antibiotics with no evacuation
complete.explain: Complete abortion requires only ecbolics (uterotonics) and antibiotics — no evacuation is needed since the cavity is empty.

incomplete.q: In incomplete abortion, the uterus is:
incomplete.explain: The uterus is smaller than the period of amenorrhea because some products have already been passed.

incomplete.q: The cervix in incomplete abortion is:
incomplete.explain: In incomplete abortion, the cervix is open with continuous bleeding, requiring evacuation.

options: Products of conception retained inside the cervical canal
cervical.explain: In cervical abortion, the products of conception are separated from the uterus but retained inside the cervical canal.

missed.q: A classic symptom of missed abortion is:
missed.explain: Missed abortion presents with regression of pregnancy symptoms, prune juice discharge (dark brown), and absent FHS.

missed.q: In missed abortion >12 weeks, treatment is:
options: Medical evacuation with prostaglandins or oxytocin
missed.explain: For missed abortion less than 12 weeks: surgical evacuation. Greater than 12 weeks: medical evacuation (prostaglandins or oxytocin).

missed.q: After fetal death in missed abortion, the pregnancy test becomes negative within:
missed.explain: Pregnancy test becomes negative within two weeks from ovum death in missed abortion.

septic.explain: Septic abortion is any type of abortion complicated by infection, which can be life-threatening.

septic.q: The immediate priority in managing septic abortion is:
options: Resuscitation and IV broad-spectrum antibiotics before evacuation
septic.explain: Resuscitation and IV broad-spectrum antibiotics must be started before evacuation. Hysterectomy is the last resort to save life.

septic.q: Positioning for a patient with septic abortion is:
septic.explain: The patient should rest in a semi-sitting position with observation for vital signs.

options: Abortion induced for a non-medical reason
Abortion induced for a medical indication
other.explain: Therapeutic abortion is induced for a medical indication, while criminal abortion is illegal and for non-medical reasons.

options: Three or more consecutive spontaneous abortions
recurrent.explain: Habitual abortion is defined as three or more consecutive spontaneous abortions.

recurrent.q: Causes of recurrent abortion include all EXCEPT:
recurrent.explain: Recurrent abortion causes include cervical incompetence (painless mid-trimester loss), uterine anomalies, chromosomal abnormalities, and APLS.

management.q: General rules for managing abortion include all EXCEPT:
options: Shock: treat its cause with resuscitation
Hemorrhage: stop bleeding and replace blood loss
Always perform immediate evacuation regardless of type
management.explain: Management depends on type. Threatened abortion is conservative (no evacuation). General rules include treat shock, stop hemorrhage, give analgesics, and evacuate when indicated.
